conversation.title: How does proximity to hazardous waste facilities influence spatial patterns of a...

messages.content: How does proximity to hazardous waste facilities influence spatial patterns of asthma prevalence across census tracts of North Carolina, and how do these relationships vary spatially by socioeconomic and demographic context?

{
  "objectives": [
    {
      "number": 1,
      "title": "Assess whether asthma prevalence is spatially clustered across North Carolina census tracts and whether hazardous-waste proximity aligns with high-asthma clusters",
      "hypothesis": "Census tracts closer to hazardous waste sites (lower nearest-site distance and/or higher site counts within 5 km) exhibit higher asthma prevalence and are overrepresented in statistically significant high-asthma clusters.",
      "data_requirements": [
        {
          "source_id": "NC_tract",
          "description": "Census tract polygon boundaries and tract identifiers (GEOID) for North Carolina.",
          "suitability_score": 10,
          "scale_note": {
            "data_scale": "tract",
            "question_scale": "tract",
            "mismatch": false,
            "resolution": "none"
          },
          "data_preprocessing": {
            "required": true,
            "rationale": "Tract geometries alone do not contain asthma prevalence or hazardous-waste proximity; proximity metrics must be derived by combining tract polygons with hazardous waste point locations, and asthma prevalence must be joined by GEOID.",
            "sources": ["NC_tract", "NC_HW_sites", "NC_PLACES"],
            "what_each_contributes": {
              "NC_tract": "tract polygons and GEOID for spatial weights and mapping",
              "NC_HW_sites": "hazardous waste point locations (geometry) for distance and buffer counts",
              "NC_PLACES": "tract-level asthma prevalence (Asthma_prev) to analyze spatial patterns"
            },
            "method": "Derive tract centroids from NC_tract geometry; compute proximity metrics to NC_HW_sites; left-join NC_PLACES to tracts by GEOID."
          },
          "data_gap": false
        }
      ],
      "steps": [
        {
          "step_number": 1,
          "operation_type": "geoprocessing",
          "operation": "reproject_layer",
          "input_nodes": ["NC_tract", "NC_HW_sites"],
          "output_node": "nc_tract_hw_projected.gpkg",
          "description": "Reproject NC_tract and NC_HW_sites to a common projected CRS suitable for distance (meters), preserving GEOID in tracts and SITE_NAME in sites.",
          "expected_output": "A GeoPackage containing projected tract polygons and projected hazardous-waste points in the same CRS for valid distance calculations."
        },
        {
          "step_number": 2,
          "operation_type": "geoprocessing",
          "operation": "centroid",
          "input_nodes": ["nc_tract_hw_projected.gpkg"],
          "output_node": "nc_tract_centroids.gpkg",
          "description": "Compute tract centroids from NC_tract geometry and keep GEOID as the unique identifier for each centroid feature.",
          "expected_output": "A GeoPackage of tract centroid points keyed by GEOID."
        },
        {
          "step_number": 3,
          "operation_type": "geoprocessing",
          "operation": "nearest_neighbor_distance",
          "input_nodes": ["nc_tract_centroids.gpkg", "nc_tract_hw_projected.gpkg"],
          "output_node": "tract_proximity_metrics.csv",
          "description": "Calculate for each GEOID the Euclidean distance (meters) from tract centroid to the nearest NC_HW_sites point and also count NC_HW_sites points within a 5000-meter buffer of each centroid.",
          "expected_output": "A CSV keyed by GEOID with proximity metrics (e.g., nearest_hw_dist_m and hw_count_5km)."
        },
        {
          "step_number": 4,
          "operation_type": "data_wrangling",
          "operation": "left_join",
          "input_nodes": ["NC_PLACES", "tract_proximity_metrics.csv"],
          "output_node": "asthma_proximity.csv",
          "description": "Left-join NC_PLACES to tract_proximity_metrics.csv on GEOID to create a table containing GEOID, Asthma_prev, nearest_hw_dist_m, and hw_count_5km.",
          "expected_output": "A tract-keyed table of asthma prevalence and hazardous-waste proximity metrics."
        },
        {
          "step_number": 5,
          "operation_type": "statistical",
          "operation": "global_morans_I",
          "input_nodes": ["nc_tract_hw_projected.gpkg", "asthma_proximity.csv"],
          "output_node": "global_moransI_asthma.json",
          "description": "Compute Global Moran’s I for Asthma_prev using tract contiguity weights (queen) after joining Asthma_prev to NC_tract by GEOID, and report permutation-based p-values.",
          "expected_output": "A JSON report of Moran’s I statistic, expected value, z-score, and permutation p-value for Asthma_prev."
        },
        {
          "step_number": 6,
          "operation_type": "statistical",
          "operation": "getis_ord_gi_star",
          "input_nodes": ["nc_tract_hw_projected.gpkg", "asthma_proximity.csv"],
          "output_node": "asthma_hotspots.gpkg",
          "description": "Compute Getis-Ord Gi* hotspot statistics for Asthma_prev using tract contiguity weights (queen) after joining Asthma_prev to NC_tract by GEOID, and store Gi* z-scores and significance classes per tract.",
          "expected_output": "A tract GeoPackage with Gi* z-scores and hotspot/coldspot classification for Asthma_prev."
        },
        {
          "step_number": 7,
          "operation_type": "statistical",
          "operation": "mann_whitney_u_test",
          "input_nodes": ["asthma_hotspots.gpkg", "asthma_proximity.csv"],
          "output_node": "hotspot_proximity_comparison.json",
          "description": "Compare nearest_hw_dist_m and hw_count_5km between statistically significant asthma hotspots (Gi* high-high at p<0.05) and non-hotspot tracts using Mann–Whitney U tests.",
          "expected_output": "A JSON summary of group medians and Mann–Whitney U test statistics and p-values for proximity differences between hotspot and non-hotspot tracts."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "number": 2,
      "title": "Determine how the proximity–asthma relationship varies by tract socioeconomic and demographic context and whether the relationship is spatially non-stationary",
      "hypothesis": "The association between hazardous-waste proximity and asthma prevalence is stronger in tracts with higher ratio_poverty, higher ratio_uninsured, and higher ratio_black, and the proximity coefficient varies spatially across North Carolina.",
      "data_requirements": [
        {
          "source_id": "NC_ACS_data",
          "description": "Tract-level socioeconomic and demographic covariates (ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, ratio_hispanic, etc.) keyed by GEOID.",
          "suitability_score": 9,
          "scale_note": {
            "data_scale": "tract",
            "question_scale": "tract",
            "mismatch": false,
            "resolution": "none"
          },
          "data_preprocessing": {
            "required": true,
            "rationale": "Modeling effect modification and spatial non-stationarity requires combining asthma and proximity metrics with socioeconomic/demographic covariates in a single GEOID-keyed tract dataset; no single source contains all required variables.",
            "sources": ["NC_ACS_data", "asthma_proximity.csv", "nc_tract_centroids.gpkg"],
            "what_each_contributes": {
              "NC_ACS_data": "context variables (ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, race/ethnicity and sex ratios)",
              "asthma_proximity.csv": "Asthma_prev and proximity metrics (nearest_hw_dist_m, hw_count_5km)",
              "nc_tract_centroids.gpkg": "centroid coordinates needed for geographically weighted regression"
            },
            "method": "Left-join NC_ACS_data to asthma_proximity.csv by GEOID; attach centroid coordinates from nc_tract_centroids.gpkg by GEOID."
          },
          "data_gap": false
        }
      ],
      "steps": [
        {
          "step_number": 1,
          "operation_type": "data_wrangling",
          "operation": "left_join",
          "input_nodes": ["asthma_proximity.csv", "NC_ACS_data"],
          "output_node": "tract_asthma_prox_acs.csv",
          "description": "Left-join NC_ACS_data to asthma_proximity.csv on GEOID to create a modeling table containing Asthma_prev, nearest_hw_dist_m, hw_count_5km, ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, ratio_hispanic, and other available ratios.",
          "expected_output": "A GEOID-keyed tract table containing outcome, exposure/proximity, and socioeconomic/demographic covariates."
        },
        {
          "step_number": 2,
          "operation_type": "data_wrangling",
          "operation": "filter_rows",
          "input_nodes": ["tract_asthma_prox_acs.csv"],
          "output_node": "tract_modeling_completecases.csv",
          "description": "Filter to complete cases for Asthma_prev, nearest_hw_dist_m, ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, and ratio_hispanic, and retain GEOID for later spatial joins.",
          "expected_output": "A cleaned modeling table with consistent sample size across all variables used in models."
        },
        {
          "step_number": 3,
          "operation_type": "data_wrangling",
          "operation": "feature_engineering",
          "input_nodes": ["tract_modeling_completecases.csv"],
          "output_node": "tract_modeling_features.csv",
          "description": "Create derived features including ln_nearest_hw_dist_m = ln(nearest_hw_dist_m), z-scored covariates for ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, and interaction terms ln_nearest_hw_dist_m:ratio_poverty and ln_nearest_hw_dist_m:ratio_black.",
          "expected_output": "A modeling-ready table with transformed proximity measures, standardized covariates, and specified interaction terms."
        },
        {
          "step_number": 4,
          "operation_type": "statistical",
          "operation": "ordinary_least_squares_with_interactions",
          "input_nodes": ["tract_modeling_features.csv"],
          "output_node": "ols_interaction_results.json",
          "description": "Fit an OLS model with Asthma_prev as the dependent variable and predictors ln_nearest_hw_dist_m, ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, ratio_hispanic, plus interaction terms ln_nearest_hw_dist_m:ratio_poverty and ln_nearest_hw_dist_m:ratio_black, and report heteroskedasticity-robust (HC) standard errors.",
          "expected_output": "A JSON model summary with coefficients, robust standard errors, p-values, and model fit metrics."
        },
        {
          "step_number": 5,
          "operation_type": "data_wrangling",
          "operation": "attach_coordinates",
          "input_nodes": ["tract_modeling_features.csv", "nc_tract_centroids.gpkg"],
          "output_node": "tract_modeling_with_xy.csv",
          "description": "Join centroid coordinates (x, y) from nc_tract_centroids.gpkg to tract_modeling_features.csv by GEOID for use in geographically weighted regression.",
          "expected_output": "A CSV containing all modeling features plus centroid x/y coordinates."
        },
        {
          "step_number": 6,
          "operation_type": "statistical",
          "operation": "geographically_weighted_regression",
          "input_nodes": ["tract_modeling_with_xy.csv"],
          "output_node": "gwr_local_coefficients.gpkg",
          "description": "Fit a geographically weighted regression (adaptive bandwidth selected by AICc; bisquare kernel) with Asthma_prev as the dependent variable and predictors ln_nearest_hw_dist_m, ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, and ratio_hispanic, and output local coefficients and local R2 at each tract centroid.",
          "expected_output": "A GeoPackage of tract centroids with local coefficient surfaces (including the local ln_nearest_hw_dist_m coefficient) and local R2 for diagnosing spatial non-stationarity."
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

import os
import geopandas as gpd

# Paths
INPUT_PATH = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/WebUI/uploads/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/NC_tract.gpkg"
OUTPUT_PATH = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/nc_tract_projected.gpkg"
OUTPUT_DIR = os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_PATH)
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

# Read
gdf = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH)

# Validate required columns exist (per inspection)
required_cols = ["GEOID", "STATEFP", "COUNTYFP", "TRACTCE", "geometry"]
missing = [c for c in required_cols if c not in gdf.columns]
if missing:
    raise KeyError(f"Missing required columns: {missing}. Available: {list(gdf.columns)}")

# Ensure GEOID preserved as string
gdf["GEOID"] = gdf["GEOID"].astype(str)

# Basic geometry validity filtering (required before spatial ops)
gdf = gdf[gdf.geometry.notnull()]
gdf = gdf[gdf.geometry.is_valid & ~gdf.geometry.is_empty].copy()

# Choose a meter-based projected CRS suitable for North Carolina distance calculations:
# EPSG:32119 = NAD83 / North Carolina (meters)
TARGET_CRS = "EPSG:32119"

# If source CRS is missing, stop (cannot reliably reproject)
if gdf.crs is None:
    raise ValueError("Input layer has no CRS defined; cannot reproject safely.")

# Reproject
gdf_proj = gdf.to_crs(TARGET_CRS)

# Write to GeoPackage
layer_name = "nc_tract_projected"
if os.path.exists(OUTPUT_PATH):
    os.remove(OUTPUT_PATH)
gdf_proj.to_file(OUTPUT_PATH, layer=layer_name, driver="GPKG")

# Record dataset for deferred visualization (optional)
record_data_to_viz(
    output_dir=OUTPUT_DIR,
    file_path=OUTPUT_PATH,
    caption="North Carolina census tract polygons reprojected to a meter-based CRS (EPSG:32119) for distance calculations, preserving GEOID.",
    viz_hint={"type": "boundary"},
    role="intermediate",
)

tracts_proj = gpd.read_file(INPUT_TRACTS_PROJ)

required_cols = {"SITE_NAME", "geometry"}
missing = required_cols - set(hw.columns)

    raise KeyError(f"Missing required columns in NC_HW_sites: {sorted(missing)}. Available: {list(hw.columns)}")

# Validate geometries

hw_proj = hw.to_crs(target_crs)

hw_proj = hw_proj[["SITE_NAME", "geometry"]].copy()

# Save
hw_proj.to_file(OUTPUT_GPKG, driver="GPKG")

record_data_to_viz(

)

```python
import os
import geopandas as gpd

INPUT_PATH = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/nc_tract_projected.gpkg"
OUTPUT_PATH = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/nc_tract_centroids.gpkg"
OUTPUT_DIR = os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_PATH)

os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

# Read projected tract polygons
tracts = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH)

# Validate geometry before spatial operations
tracts = tracts[tracts.geometry.is_valid & ~tracts.geometry.is_empty].copy()

# Ensure GEOID is present and non-null
tracts = tracts.dropna(subset=["GEOID"]).copy()
tracts["GEOID"] = tracts["GEOID"].astype(str)

# Compute centroids (input is projected, so planar centroids are appropriate)
centroids = tracts[["GEOID", "geometry"]].copy()
centroids["geometry"] = centroids.geometry.centroid
centroids = gpd.GeoDataFrame(centroids, geometry="geometry", crs=tracts.crs)

# Save centroid points to GeoPackage
centroids.to_file(OUTPUT_PATH, driver="GPKG", layer="nc_tract_centroids")

# Optional: register as data-to-viz for downstream auto-rendering
record_data_to_viz(
    output_dir=OUTPUT_DIR,
    file_path=OUTPUT_PATH,
    caption="Centroid point locations for North Carolina Census tracts keyed by GEOID.",
    viz_hint={"type": "point"},
    role="intermediate",
)

## Canonical example (CSV → GeoPackage left join on GEOID, with audit)

```python
import os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd

# ---- 1. Load ------------------------------------------------------------
left  = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH_LEFT)   # polygons (or a GeoDataFrame)
right = pd.read_csv(INPUT_PATH_RIGHT)    # attribute CSV

LEFT_KEY  = "<GEOID_COLUMN_ON_LEFT>"     # e.g. "GEOID"
RIGHT_KEY = "<GEOID_COLUMN_ON_RIGHT>"    # e.g. "GEOID"
KEY_WIDTH = 5                            # 2=state, 3=county, 5=county-GEOID, 11=tract

# ---- 2. Normalize join keys on BOTH sides -------------------------------
left[LEFT_KEY]   = left[LEFT_KEY].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(KEY_WIDTH)
right[RIGHT_KEY] = right[RIGHT_KEY].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(KEY_WIDTH)

# ---- 3. Clean Census sentinels on the right side ------------------------
for col in right.select_dtypes(include=["number", "object"]).columns:
    right[col] = pd.to_numeric(right[col], errors="ignore")
right = right.replace([-666666666, -888888888, -999999999], np.nan)

# ---- 4. Left join -------------------------------------------------------
merged = left.merge(right, left_on=LEFT_KEY, right_on=RIGHT_KEY, how="left")

# ---- 5. AUDIT the merge -------------------------------------------------
right_added = [c for c in right.columns if c != RIGHT_KEY and c in merged.columns]
audit_col   = right_added[0] if right_added else None

if audit_col is not None:
    matched      = int(merged[audit_col].notna().sum())
    total        = len(merged)
    match_rate   = matched / total if total else 0.0
    unmatched_n  = total - matched

    audit_lines = [
        f"[JOIN AUDIT] merged {total} left rows",
        f"  matched on '{audit_col}': {matched}/{total} ({match_rate:.1%})",
        f"  unmatched (NaN in right side): {unmatched_n}",
    ]
    if match_rate < 0.9:
        audit_lines.append(f"  WARNING: match rate below 90% — inspect keys:")
        audit_lines.append(f"    left  {LEFT_KEY} sample: {left[LEFT_KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
        audit_lines.append(f"    right {RIGHT_KEY} sample: {right[RIGHT_KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
        missing = merged.loc[merged[audit_col].isna(), LEFT_KEY].head(10).tolist()
        audit_lines.append(f"    example unmatched left keys: {missing}")
    audit = "\n".join(audit_lines)
    print(audit)
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    with open(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "join_audit.txt"), "w") as f:
        f.write(audit + "\n")

# ---- 6. Save primary output --------------------------------------------
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH), exist_ok=True)
if isinstance(merged, gpd.GeoDataFrame):
    merged.to_file(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH, driver="GPKG")
else:
    merged.to_csv(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH, index=False)
```

TRACT_CENTROIDS_PATH = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/nc_tract_centroids.gpkg"
HW_SITES_PATH = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/nc_hw_sites_projected.gpkg"
OUTPUT_CSV_PATH = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/tract_proximity_metrics.csv"

os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_CSV_PATH), exist_ok=True)
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

# -------------------------
# Load

tract = gpd.read_file(TRACT_CENTROIDS_PATH)
hw = gpd.read_file(HW_SITES_PATH)

# -------------------------
# Validate required columns (per inspected schemas)
# -------------------------
required_tract_cols = {"GEOID", "geometry"}
required_hw_cols = {"SITE_NAME", "geometry"}

missing_tract = required_tract_cols - set(tract.columns)
missing_hw = required_hw_cols - set(hw.columns)

if missing_tract:
    raise KeyError(f"Missing columns in tract centroids: {sorted(missing_tract)}; available={list(tract.columns)}")
if missing_hw:
    raise KeyError(f"Missing columns in HW sites: {sorted(missing_hw)}; available={list(hw.columns)}")

# -------------------------
# Clean / validate geometry
# -------------------------
tract = tract[tract.geometry.notna()]
hw = hw[hw.geometry.notna()]

tract = tract[tract.geometry.is_valid & ~tract.geometry.is_empty].copy()
hw = hw[hw.geometry.is_valid & ~hw.geometry.is_empty].copy()

tract["GEOID"] = tract["GEOID"].astype(str).str.strip()

# -------------------------
# CRS harmonization: ensure projected CRS with meter units
# -------------------------
target_crs = tract.crs
if target_crs is None or (hasattr(target_crs, "is_geographic") and target_crs.is_geographic):
    target_crs = "EPSG:3857"  # meter-based fallback if input is geographic/undefined

tract = tract.to_crs(target_crs)
hw = hw.to_crs(target_crs)

# -------------------------
# Metrics
#   - nearest_hw_dist_m: nearest Euclidean distance from centroid to nearest HW point
#   - hw_count_5km: count of HW points within 5000 m
# -------------------------
# Nearest distance
if len(hw) == 0 or len(tract) == 0:
    out = pd.DataFrame(
        {
            "GEOID": tract["GEOID"].values if len(tract) else [],
            "nearest_hw_dist_m": np.nan,
            "hw_count_5km": 0,
        }
    )
else:
    nearest = gpd.sjoin_nearest(
        tract[["GEOID", "geometry"]],
        hw[["SITE_NAME", "geometry"]],
        how="left",
        distance_col="nearest_hw_dist_m",
    )

    dist_df = nearest[["GEOID", "nearest_hw_dist_m"]].copy()

    # Count within 5km
    tract_buf = tract[["GEOID", "geometry"]].copy()
    tract_buf["geometry"] = tract_buf.geometry.buffer(5000)

    within = gpd.sjoin(
        tract_buf,
        hw[["SITE_NAME", "geometry"]],
        how="left",
        predicate="intersects",
    )

    count_df = (
        within.groupby("GEOID", dropna=False)
        .size()
        .rename("hw_count_5km")
        .reset_index()
    )

    # If a tract had no matches in left join, size() returns 1 (the left row) with NaNs on right.
    # Correct by counting only non-null right-side matches.
    # (In geopandas sjoin, right columns appear; we have SITE_NAME from right.)
    count_df = (
        within.assign(_has_match=within["SITE_NAME"].notna().astype(int))
        .groupby("GEOID", dropna=False)["_has_match"]
        .sum()
        .rename("hw_count_5km")
        .reset_index()
    )

    out = dist_df.merge(count_df, on="GEOID", how="left")
    out["hw_count_5km"] = out["hw_count_5km"].fillna(0).astype(int)

# -------------------------
# Save
# -------------------------
out.to_csv(OUTPUT_CSV_PATH, index=False)

# Data Merging (Join, Merge, Spatial Join)

## Description

Combine two tables by a shared key (`pd.merge`) or by geometry (`gpd.sjoin`).
The correctness of every downstream step depends on the **match rate** — a
silent join that produces NaN for 50% of rows will poison later modeling.

## When to use

- Linking attribute data (CSV) to polygons (GeoPackage/SHP) on FIPS/GEOID.
- Combining variables from two CSV sources on a shared key.
- Enriching points/polygons via spatial containment.

## When NOT to use

- Reshape only (pivot/melt) — not a merge.
- Concatenating rows of identical schema — use `pd.concat`.

## Critical constraints

1. **Always cast join keys to string and `zfill` to a consistent width**
   before merging. Int-vs-string and missing-leading-zero mismatches silently
   drop every match.
   - State FIPS: `zfill(2)` (e.g. `"06"`).
   - County FIPS: `zfill(3)` (e.g. `"001"`).
   - County GEOID (state+county): `zfill(5)` (e.g. `"06001"`).
   - Tract GEOID: `zfill(11)`.
2. **Strip whitespace** on both keys: `.astype(str).str.strip()`.
3. **Replace Census sentinels with NaN** on any ACS/Decennial column:
   `df.replace(-666666666, np.nan, inplace=True)`. Values like `-999999999`,
   `-888888888`, `-666666666` are Census "suppressed / not available" codes
   — not real numbers.
4. **Audit and print match rate** after every merge. A match rate below 90%
   is a red flag — either keys are mis-formatted or the right-side dataset
   doesn't cover all left-side units (report both hypotheses).
5. **Left join keeps every left row** — NaN on the right side does NOT raise
   an error. You must inspect the output for NaN, not just row count.
6. **For spatial joins, both GeoDataFrames must be in the same projected
   CRS.** Reproject before `sjoin`.

## Paths

- `INPUT_PATH_LEFT`, `INPUT_PATH_RIGHT` — from the step's Input Node Paths.
- `OUTPUT_NODE_PATH` — merged primary output.
- `OUTPUT_DIR` — directory for join-audit log.

Never hardcode `"data.csv"`, `"dataset_a.csv"`, etc.

## Canonical example (CSV → GeoPackage left join on GEOID, with audit)

```python
import os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd

# ---- 1. Load ------------------------------------------------------------
left  = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH_LEFT)   # polygons (or a GeoDataFrame)
right = pd.read_csv(INPUT_PATH_RIGHT)    # attribute CSV

LEFT_KEY  = "<GEOID_COLUMN_ON_LEFT>"     # e.g. "GEOID"
RIGHT_KEY = "<GEOID_COLUMN_ON_RIGHT>"    # e.g. "GEOID"
KEY_WIDTH = 5                            # 2=state, 3=county, 5=county-GEOID, 11=tract

# ---- 2. Normalize join keys on BOTH sides -------------------------------
left[LEFT_KEY]   = left[LEFT_KEY].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(KEY_WIDTH)
right[RIGHT_KEY] = right[RIGHT_KEY].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(KEY_WIDTH)

# ---- 3. Clean Census sentinels on the right side ------------------------
for col in right.select_dtypes(include=["number", "object"]).columns:
    right[col] = pd.to_numeric(right[col], errors="ignore")
right = right.replace([-666666666, -888888888, -999999999], np.nan)

# ---- 4. Left join -------------------------------------------------------
merged = left.merge(right, left_on=LEFT_KEY, right_on=RIGHT_KEY, how="left")

# ---- 5. AUDIT the merge -------------------------------------------------
right_added = [c for c in right.columns if c != RIGHT_KEY and c in merged.columns]
audit_col   = right_added[0] if right_added else None

if audit_col is not None:
    matched      = int(merged[audit_col].notna().sum())
    total        = len(merged)
    match_rate   = matched / total if total else 0.0
    unmatched_n  = total - matched

    audit_lines = [
        f"[JOIN AUDIT] merged {total} left rows",
        f"  matched on '{audit_col}': {matched}/{total} ({match_rate:.1%})",
        f"  unmatched (NaN in right side): {unmatched_n}",
    ]
    if match_rate < 0.9:
        audit_lines.append(f"  WARNING: match rate below 90% — inspect keys:")
        audit_lines.append(f"    left  {LEFT_KEY} sample: {left[LEFT_KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
        audit_lines.append(f"    right {RIGHT_KEY} sample: {right[RIGHT_KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
        missing = merged.loc[merged[audit_col].isna(), LEFT_KEY].head(10).tolist()
        audit_lines.append(f"    example unmatched left keys: {missing}")
    audit = "\n".join(audit_lines)
    print(audit)
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    with open(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "join_audit.txt"), "w") as f:
        f.write(audit + "\n")

# ---- 6. Save primary output --------------------------------------------
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH), exist_ok=True)
if isinstance(merged, gpd.GeoDataFrame):
    merged.to_file(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH, driver="GPKG")
else:
    merged.to_csv(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH, index=False)
```

## Spatial join variant (points in polygons)

```python
import geopandas as gpd

points   = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH_LEFT)
polygons = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH_RIGHT)

# Reproject to same CRS (use polygons' CRS by convention)
if points.crs != polygons.crs:
    points = points.to_crs(polygons.crs)

joined = gpd.sjoin(points, polygons, how="left", predicate="within")

# Audit — how many points landed in a polygon?
unmatched = joined["index_right"].isna().sum()
print(f"[SJOIN AUDIT] {len(joined)-unmatched}/{len(joined)} points matched a polygon "
      f"({unmatched} outside all polygons)")
```

## Common pitfalls

| Pitfall | Fix |
|---|---|
| Int vs string FIPS silently produces 0 matches | Cast both sides to `str.strip().str.zfill(W)` with the right width. |
| Leading zeros stripped by `pd.read_csv` | Either `dtype={'GEOID': str}` at read time, or zfill after load. |
| ACS sentinel `-666666666` treated as a real value | `df.replace([-666666666, -888888888, -999999999], np.nan)`. |
| "58 records created" looks fine but half are NaN | Always print match rate, not just row count. |
| `sjoin` returns 0 rows | Reproject to a common CRS first; verify with `points.within(polygons.unary_union).sum()`. |
| `how="inner"` after a previously-failed join | Use `how="left"` for auditing; switch to inner only after you've verified the match rate. |
| Duplicated keys on the right side explode left row count | `right.drop_duplicates(subset=[RIGHT_KEY])` before merge if right is supposed to be unique. |
| Tract vs county GEOID width mismatch | Decide which level the join is at; enforce the matching width on both sides. |

--- Reading skill doc: 'join CSV to GeoPackage by key GEOID geopandas merge validate join keys' ---

# Data Merging (Join, Merge, Spatial Join)

## Description

Combine two tables by a shared key (`pd.merge`) or by geometry (`gpd.sjoin`).
The correctness of every downstream step depends on the **match rate** — a
silent join that produces NaN for 50% of rows will poison later modeling.

## When to use

- Linking attribute data (CSV) to polygons (GeoPackage/SHP) on FIPS/GEOID.
- Combining variables from two CSV sources on a shared key.
- Enriching points/polygons via spatial containment.

## When NOT to use

- Reshape only (pivot/melt) — not a merge.
- Concatenating rows of identical schema — use `pd.concat`.

## Critical constraints

1. **Always cast join keys to string and `zfill` to a consistent width**
   before merging. Int-vs-string and missing-leading-zero mismatches silently
   drop every match.
   - State FIPS: `zfill(2)` (e.g. `"06"`).
   - County FIPS: `zfill(3)` (e.g. `"001"`).
   - County GEOID (state+county): `zfill(5)` (e.g. `"06001"`).
   - Tract GEOID: `zfill(11)`.
2. **Strip whitespace** on both keys: `.astype(str).str.strip()`.
3. **Replace Census sentinels with NaN** on any ACS/Decennial column:
   `df.replace(-666666666, np.nan, inplace=True)`. Values like `-999999999`,
   `-888888888`, `-666666666` are Census "suppressed / not available" codes
   — not real numbers.
4. **Audit and print match rate** after every merge. A match rate below 90%
   is a red flag — either keys are mis-formatted or the right-side dataset
   doesn't cover all left-side units (report both hypotheses).
5. **Left join keeps every left row** — NaN on the right side does NOT raise
   an error. You must inspect the output for NaN, not just row count.
6. **For spatial joins, both GeoDataFrames must be in the same projected
   CRS.** Reproject before `sjoin`.

## Paths

- `INPUT_PATH_LEFT`, `INPUT_PATH_RIGHT` — from the step's Input Node Paths.
- `OUTPUT_NODE_PATH` — merged primary output.
- `OUTPUT_DIR` — directory for join-audit log.

Never hardcode `"data.csv"`, `"dataset_a.csv"`, etc.

## Canonical example (CSV → GeoPackage left join on GEOID, with audit)

```python
import os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd

# ---- 1. Load ------------------------------------------------------------
left  = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH_LEFT)   # polygons (or a GeoDataFrame)
right = pd.read_csv(INPUT_PATH_RIGHT)    # attribute CSV

LEFT_KEY  = "<GEOID_COLUMN_ON_LEFT>"     # e.g. "GEOID"
RIGHT_KEY = "<GEOID_COLUMN_ON_RIGHT>"    # e.g. "GEOID"
KEY_WIDTH = 5                            # 2=state, 3=county, 5=county-GEOID, 11=tract

# ---- 2. Normalize join keys on BOTH sides -------------------------------
left[LEFT_KEY]   = left[LEFT_KEY].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(KEY_WIDTH)
right[RIGHT_KEY] = right[RIGHT_KEY].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(KEY_WIDTH)

# ---- 3. Clean Census sentinels on the right side ------------------------
for col in right.select_dtypes(include=["number", "object"]).columns:
    right[col] = pd.to_numeric(right[col], errors="ignore")
right = right.replace([-666666666, -888888888, -999999999], np.nan)

# ---- 4. Left join -------------------------------------------------------
merged = left.merge(right, left_on=LEFT_KEY, right_on=RIGHT_KEY, how="left")

# ---- 5. AUDIT the merge -------------------------------------------------
right_added = [c for c in right.columns if c != RIGHT_KEY and c in merged.columns]
audit_col   = right_added[0] if right_added else None

if audit_col is not None:
    matched      = int(merged[audit_col].notna().sum())
    total        = len(merged)
    match_rate   = matched / total if total else 0.0
    unmatched_n  = total - matched

    audit_lines = [
        f"[JOIN AUDIT] merged {total} left rows",
        f"  matched on '{audit_col}': {matched}/{total} ({match_rate:.1%})",
        f"  unmatched (NaN in right side): {unmatched_n}",
    ]
    if match_rate < 0.9:
        audit_lines.append(f"  WARNING: match rate below 90% — inspect keys:")
        audit_lines.append(f"    left  {LEFT_KEY} sample: {left[LEFT_KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
        audit_lines.append(f"    right {RIGHT_KEY} sample: {right[RIGHT_KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
        missing = merged.loc[merged[audit_col].isna(), LEFT_KEY].head(10).tolist()
        audit_lines.append(f"    example unmatched left keys: {missing}")
    audit = "\n".join(audit_lines)
    print(audit)
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    with open(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "join_audit.txt"), "w") as f:
        f.write(audit + "\n")

# ---- 6. Save primary output --------------------------------------------
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH), exist_ok=True)
if isinstance(merged, gpd.GeoDataFrame):
    merged.to_file(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH, driver="GPKG")
else:
    merged.to_csv(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH, index=False)
```

## Spatial join variant (points in polygons)

```python
import geopandas as gpd

points   = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH_LEFT)
polygons = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH_RIGHT)

# Reproject to same CRS (use polygons' CRS by convention)
if points.crs != polygons.crs:
    points = points.to_crs(polygons.crs)

joined = gpd.sjoin(points, polygons, how="left", predicate="within")

# Audit — how many points landed in a polygon?
unmatched = joined["index_right"].isna().sum()
print(f"[SJOIN AUDIT] {len(joined)-unmatched}/{len(joined)} points matched a polygon "
      f"({unmatched} outside all polygons)")
```

## Common pitfalls

| Pitfall | Fix |
|---|---|
| Int vs string FIPS silently produces 0 matches | Cast both sides to `str.strip().str.zfill(W)` with the right width. |
| Leading zeros stripped by `pd.read_csv` | Either `dtype={'GEOID': str}` at read time, or zfill after load. |
| ACS sentinel `-666666666` treated as a real value | `df.replace([-666666666, -888888888, -999999999], np.nan)`. |
| "58 records created" looks fine but half are NaN | Always print match rate, not just row count. |
| `sjoin` returns 0 rows | Reproject to a common CRS first; verify with `points.within(polygons.unary_union).sum()`. |
| `how="inner"` after a previously-failed join | Use `how="left"` for auditing; switch to inner only after you've verified the match rate. |
| Duplicated keys on the right side explode left row count | `right.drop_duplicates(subset=[RIGHT_KEY])` before merge if right is supposed to be unique. |
| Tract vs county GEOID width mismatch | Decide which level the join is at; enforce the matching width on both sides. |

OUTPUT_DIR = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311"

# -------------------------

# -------------------------
# STEP 2: LOOKUP SKILL DOC

# Validate input existence
for p in [INPUT_GPKG, INPUT_CSV]:
    if not os.path.exists(p):
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Missing required input: {p}")

# Load data

prox = pd.read_csv(INPUT_CSV)

# Validate / clean geometry before any spatial operations (even though we do attribute join)
tracts = tracts[tracts.geometry.notna()]
tracts = tracts[tracts.geometry.is_valid & ~tracts.geometry.is_empty].copy()

# Normalize join keys (tract GEOID width = 11)
KEY = "GEOID"
KEY_WIDTH = 11

tracts[KEY] = tracts[KEY].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(KEY_WIDTH)
prox[KEY] = prox[KEY].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(KEY_WIDTH)

# Keep only needed columns from CSV (avoid accidental column collisions)
needed_cols = [KEY, "Asthma_prev", "nearest_hw_dist_m", "hw_count_5km"]
missing = [c for c in needed_cols if c not in prox.columns]
if missing:
    raise KeyError(f"Missing required columns in asthma_proximity.csv: {missing}. Available: {list(prox.columns)}")
prox = prox[needed_cols].copy()

# Coerce numeric fields (CSV columns came in as object)
for c in ["Asthma_prev", "nearest_hw_dist_m", "hw_count_5km"]:
    prox[c] = pd.to_numeric(prox[c], errors="coerce")

# Replace common sentinel missing codes (defensive)
prox = prox.replace([-666666666, -888888888, -999999999], np.nan)

# Ensure right-side GEOID is unique (avoid exploding row counts)
dup_mask = prox[KEY].duplicated(keep=False)
if dup_mask.any():
    # Keep the first occurrence; could also aggregate, but operation requests a direct join.
    prox = prox.drop_duplicates(subset=[KEY], keep="first").copy()

# Left join: keep all tract polygons
merged = tracts.merge(prox, on=KEY, how="left")

# Join audit
audit_col = "Asthma_prev"
matched = int(merged[audit_col].notna().sum())
total = len(merged)
match_rate = matched / total if total else 0.0
unmatched = total - matched

audit_lines = [
    "[JOIN AUDIT] Tract polygons enriched with asthma/proximity attributes",
    f"  left rows (tracts): {total}",
    f"  matched rows (non-null {audit_col}): {matched}/{total} ({match_rate:.1%})",
    f"  unmatched rows: {unmatched}",
]
if total and match_rate < 0.9:
    audit_lines.append("  WARNING: match rate below 90% — inspect GEOID formatting/coverage.")
    audit_lines.append(f"    left GEOID sample: {tracts[KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
    audit_lines.append(f"    right GEOID sample: {prox[KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
    audit_lines.append(
        f"    example unmatched left GEOIDs: {merged.loc[merged[audit_col].isna(), KEY].head(10).tolist()}"
    )

audit_text = "\n".join(audit_lines)
print(audit_text)
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
with open(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "join_audit_tract_asthma_proximity.txt"), "w") as f:
    f.write(audit_text + "\n")

# Save output GeoPackage
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_GPKG), exist_ok=True)
merged.to_file(OUTPUT_GPKG, driver="GPKG")

# Record as data-to-viz artifact for downstream map rendering (choropleth-ready)

    output_dir=OUTPUT_DIR,
    file_path=OUTPUT_GPKG,
    caption="North Carolina tract polygons joined with asthma prevalence and highway proximity attributes.",

)

    f"  matched rows (non-null {audit_col}): {matched}/{total} ({match_rate:.1%})",
    f"  unmatched rows: {unmatched}",
]
if total and match_rate < 0.9:
    audit_lines.append("  WARNING: match rate below 90% — inspect GEOID formatting/coverage.")
    audit_lines.append(f"    left GEOID sample: {tracts[KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
    audit_lines.append(f"    right GEOID sample: {prox[KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
    audit_lines.append(
        f"    example unmatched left GEOIDs: {merged.loc[merged[audit_col].isna(), KEY].head(10).tolist()}"
    )

audit_text = "\n".join(audit_lines)
print(audit_text)
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
with open(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "join_audit_tract_asthma_proximity.txt"), "w") as f:
    f.write(audit_text + "\n")

# Save output GeoPackage
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_GPKG), exist_ok=True)
merged.to_file(OUTPUT_GPKG, driver="GPKG")

# Global Moran's I

## Description

Global Moran's I is a single-number test: **is there overall spatial autocorrelation in this variable?** Positive I means similar values cluster together; negative I means neighbors differ; I near E[I] means random.

## When to use

- Screening a variable for spatial clustering before further spatial analysis.
- Checking **regression residuals** — significant Moran's I on residuals justifies moving from OLS to spatial regression or GWR.
- Comparing clustering strength across multiple variables or time periods.

## When NOT to use

- Locating **where** clusters are → use Local Moran's I (LISA).
- Testing two-variable relationships → bivariate correlation skill.
- Variable with no spatial geometry attached.

## Critical constraints

1. **Build weights on the cleaned GeoDataFrame** — drop NAs first, then `Queen.from_dataframe(gdf)`.
2. **Row-standardize** — `w.transform = "r"`.
3. **Use permutations for inference** — `permutations=999` gives stable p-values; don't rely on the analytical z-score alone.
4. **Prefer KNN if the Queen graph has islands** — see the Spatial Weights skill.

## Paths

- `INPUT_PATH` — GeoPackage/SHP from Input Node Paths.
- `OUTPUT_NODE_PATH` — CSV with Moran's I statistics (primary result).
- `OUTPUT_DIR` — for any secondary artifacts if added.

Never hardcode `"data.gpkg"`.

## Canonical example (variable + residuals)

```python
import os
import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd
from libpysal.weights import Queen, KNN
from esda.moran import Moran

# INPUT_PATH / OUTPUT_NODE_PATH from step I/O section.
gdf = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH)

col = "<TARGET_COLUMN>"          # or a residual column like "gwr_resid"
gdf = gdf.dropna(subset=[col]).reset_index(drop=True)

# ---- Guard against empty / tiny frames (usually caused by upstream join NaN) ----
if len(gdf) == 0:
    raise ValueError(f"No rows after dropping NA in '{col}'. Check upstream join match rates.")
if len(gdf) < 10:
    print(f"[WARN] Only {len(gdf)} rows — spatial statistics will be unreliable.")

# ---- Weights ------------------------------------------------------------
w = Queen.from_dataframe(gdf)
if w.islands:
    w = KNN.from_dataframe(gdf, k=5)
w.transform = "r"

# ---- Global Moran's I ---------------------------------------------------
mi = Moran(gdf[col].values, w, permutations=999)

print(f"Global Moran's I on '{col}':")
print(f"  I          = {mi.I:.4f}")
print(f"  E[I]       = {mi.EI:.4f}")
print(f"  z (sim)    = {mi.z_sim:.4f}")
print(f"  p (sim)    = {mi.p_sim:.6f}")
print("  =>", "significant spatial autocorrelation" if mi.p_sim < 0.05 else "no significant autocorrelation")

# ---- Save ---------------------------------------------------------------
results = pd.DataFrame([{
    "variable": col,
    "morans_I": mi.I,
    "expected_I": mi.EI,
    "variance_sim": mi.VI_sim,
    "z_sim": mi.z_sim,
    "p_sim": mi.p_sim,
    "n": len(gdf),
}])
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH), exist_ok=True)
results.to_csv(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH, index=False)
```

## Interpretation

| Condition | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `I > E[I]` and `p_sim < 0.05` | Positive spatial autocorrelation — clustering of similar values |
| `I ≈ E[I]` and `p_sim > 0.05` | Spatially random |
| `I < E[I]` and `p_sim < 0.05` | Negative spatial autocorrelation — checkerboard/dispersion |

`E[I] = -1/(n-1)` — essentially 0 for large n.

**Residual diagnostic:** if Moran's I on OLS residuals is significant, OLS is mis-specified spatially → try spatial lag/error regression or GWR.

## Common pitfalls

| Pitfall | Fix |
|---|---|
| Weights built on original gdf, analysis on subset | Drop NAs first, reset index, then build weights. |
| Forgot row-standardization | `w.transform = "r"`. |
| Islands in Queen weights | Swap in `KNN.from_dataframe(gdf, k=5)`. |
| Reporting only `mi.p_norm` | Prefer `mi.p_sim` (permutation-based) — more robust. |
| Interpreting as "where" | Global I tells you *whether*, not *where*. Follow up with LISA. |

```

OUTPUT_PATH = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/global_moransI_asthma.json"

target_col = "Asthma_prev"

if len(gdf) == 0:

if len(gdf) < 3:

# ----------------------------

w = Queen.from_dataframe(gdf, use_index=False)

permutations = 999
y = gdf[target_col].to_numpy(dtype=float)

mi = Moran(y, w, permutations=permutations)

# Prepare results

    "n": int(len(gdf)),

        "permutations": int(permutations),
    },
    "global_morans_I": {
        "I": float(mi.I),
        "expected_I": float(mi.EI),
        "z_score_permutation": float(mi.z_sim),
        "p_value_permutation": float(mi.p_sim),
        "z_score_normal": float(mi.z_norm),
        "p_value_normal": float(mi.p_norm),
        "variance_sim": float(mi.VI_sim),
        "variance_norm": float(mi.VI_norm),
    },
}

# ----------------------------
# Save output JSON
# ----------------------------
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
with open(OUTPUT_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    json.dump(results, f, indent=2)

# Spatial Weights

## Description

A spatial weights matrix `W` defines which observations are neighbors and how strongly they're connected. Almost every spatial statistic (Moran's I, LISA, spatial lag/error regression) takes `W` as input.

## When to use

- Before Global or Local Moran's I.
- Before spatial regression (SAR, SEM) or GWR diagnostics.
- Any time you need to express "neighbor of" relationships between geographic units.

## When NOT to use

- Non-spatial data — no geometry, no weights.
- Purely attribute-space neighborhoods (use sklearn KNN on features instead).

## Choosing the scheme

| Scheme | Use when | Class |
|---|---|---|
| Queen contiguity | Polygons (counties, tracts); neighbors share edge or vertex | `libpysal.weights.Queen` |
| Rook contiguity | Polygons where only edge-sharing counts | `libpysal.weights.Rook` |
| KNN | Points, or polygons with islands; fixed neighbor count `k` | `libpysal.weights.KNN` |
| Distance band | Points with meaningful metric distance | `libpysal.weights.DistanceBand` |

## Critical constraints

1. **Always row-standardize** — `w.transform = "r"` before any Moran's I.
2. **Check for islands** — isolated features break Moran's I. Switch to KNN if Queen produces them.
3. **Drop NAs first, then build weights** — weights must align with the rows actually used.
4. **Project before distance-band weights** — distances are meaningless in lat/lon degrees.

## Paths

This skill only reads data — it produces a `w` object, not a file.

- `INPUT_PATH` — GeoPackage/SHP from Input Node Paths. Never hardcode.

## Canonical example

```python
import geopandas as gpd
from libpysal.weights import Queen, KNN, DistanceBand

gdf = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH)
col = "<ANALYSIS_COLUMN>"

# ---- 1. Drop NAs on the column you'll analyze ---------------------------
gdf = gdf.dropna(subset=[col]).reset_index(drop=True)

# ---- 2. Build weights ---------------------------------------------------
# (a) Queen contiguity — default for polygons
w = Queen.from_dataframe(gdf)

# (b) KNN fallback if Queen produces islands
if w.islands:
    print(f"Queen produced {len(w.islands)} islands — switching to KNN(k=5)")
    w = KNN.from_dataframe(gdf, k=5)

# (c) Distance band — requires projected CRS in meters
# if gdf.crs.is_geographic:
#     gdf = gdf.to_crs(epsg=3857)
# w = DistanceBand.from_dataframe(gdf, threshold=10000, binary=False)

# ---- 3. Row-standardize -------------------------------------------------
w.transform = "r"

print(f"n = {w.n}, mean neighbors = {w.mean_neighbors:.2f}, islands = {len(w.islands)}")
```

## Common pitfalls

| Pitfall | Fix |
|---|---|
| Islands break Moran's I | `if w.islands: use KNN.from_dataframe(gdf, k=5)`. |
| Forgot to row-standardize | `w.transform = "r"` every time. |
| Weights built before dropping NAs | Drop first, reset index, then build — row counts must match. |
| Distance band in lat/lon | Reproject to a meter-based CRS first. |
| `k` too small in KNN | `k=5` to `k=8` is typical; too small gives noisy LISA. |

```
```python
import os
import numpy as np
import geopandas as gpd

from libpysal.weights import Queen
from esda.getisord import G_Local

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Paths
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
INPUT_PATH = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/tract_asthma_proximity.gpkg"
OUTPUT_PATH = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/asthma_hotspots.gpkg"
OUTPUT_DIR = os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_PATH)
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Load + validate
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
gdf = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH)

required_cols = ["GEOID", "Asthma_prev", "geometry"]
missing = [c for c in required_cols if c not in gdf.columns]
if missing:
    raise KeyError(f"Missing required columns: {missing}. Available columns: {list(gdf.columns)}")

# Keep valid geometries only
gdf = gdf[gdf.geometry.notnull()]
gdf = gdf[gdf.geometry.is_valid & ~gdf.geometry.is_empty].copy()

# Drop null analysis values
gdf = gdf.dropna(subset=["Asthma_prev"]).copy()

# Reset index so weights align 0..n-1
gdf = gdf.reset_index(drop=True)

# Ensure GEOID is string
gdf["GEOID"] = gdf["GEOID"].astype(str)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Queen contiguity weights (with island handling)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
w = Queen.from_dataframe(gdf)

# Handle islands while preserving Queen as the base scheme:
# give each island a self-neighbor so Gi* is defined.
if getattr(w, "islands", None):
    for i in w.islands:
        w.neighbors[i] = [i]
        w.weights[i] = [1.0]

# Row-standardize
w.transform = "r"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Getis-Ord Gi* (local G*): z-scores + permutation p-values
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
y = gdf["Asthma_prev"].to_numpy(dtype=float)
gi = G_Local(y, w, star=True, permutations=999)

# Attach results
gdf["GiZ"] = np.asarray(gi.Zs, dtype=float)
gdf["GiP"] = np.asarray(gi.p_sim, dtype=float)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Significance classification (hotspot/coldspot) based on p-value + sign(z)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
def classify_gistar(z, p):
    if not np.isfinite(z) or not np.isfinite(p):
        return "NoData"
    # Hotspots (positive z)
    if z > 0:
        if p <= 0.01:
            return "Hotspot_99"
        if p <= 0.05:
            return "Hotspot_95"
        if p <= 0.10:
            return "Hotspot_90"
        return "NotSig"
    # Coldspots (negative z)
    if z < 0:
        if p <= 0.01:
            return "Coldspot_99"
        if p <= 0.05:
            return "Coldspot_95"
        if p <= 0.10:
            return "Coldspot_90"
        return "NotSig"
    return "NotSig"

gdf["GiClass"] = [classify_gistar(z, p) for z, p in zip(gdf["GiZ"], gdf["GiP"])]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Save output
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Keep all original attributes + Gi* outputs; ensure a stable schema
out_gdf = gdf.copy()

# GeoPackage write
out_gdf.to_file(OUTPUT_PATH, driver="GPKG")

# Manuscript artifact (auto-render choropleth later)
record_data_to_viz(
    output_dir=OUTPUT_DIR,
    file_path=OUTPUT_PATH,
    caption="Census tract Getis-Ord Gi* hotspot/coldspot classification for asthma prevalence (Queen contiguity).",
    viz_hint={"type": "choropleth", "column": "GiZ", "cmap": "RdBu_r"},
    role="final",
)
```

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
y = gdf["Asthma_prev"].to_numpy(dtype=float)
gi = G_Local(y, w, star=True, permutations=999)

# Attach results
gdf["GiZ"] = np.asarray(gi.Zs, dtype=float)
gdf["GiP"] = np.asarray(gi.p_sim, dtype=float)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Significance classification (hotspot/coldspot) based on p-value + sign(z)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
def classify_gistar(z, p):
    if not np.isfinite(z) or not np.isfinite(p):
        return "NoData"
    # Hotspots (positive z)
    if z > 0:
        if p <= 0.01:
            return "Hotspot_99"
        if p <= 0.05:
            return "Hotspot_95"
        if p <= 0.10:
            return "Hotspot_90"
        return "NotSig"
    # Coldspots (negative z)
    if z < 0:
        if p <= 0.01:
            return "Coldspot_99"
        if p <= 0.05:
            return "Coldspot_95"
        if p <= 0.10:
            return "Coldspot_90"
        return "NotSig"
    return "NotSig"

gdf["GiClass"] = [classify_gistar(z, p) for z, p in zip(gdf["GiZ"], gdf["GiP"])]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Save output
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Keep all original attributes + Gi* outputs; ensure a stable schema
out_gdf = gdf.copy()

# GeoPackage write
out_gdf.to_file(OUTPUT_PATH, driver="GPKG")

import os
import json
import warnings

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd

from scipy.stats import mannwhitneyu
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Pre-imported in the environment per instructions:
# record_figure, record_table, record_data_to_viz

INPUT_GPKG = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/asthma_hotspots.gpkg"
OUTPUT_PATH = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/hotspot_proximity_comparison.json"
OUTPUT_DIR = os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_PATH)
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=UserWarning)

# ----------------------------
# Load data (handle multi-layer geopackage)
# ----------------------------
layer_to_read = None
try:
    import fiona
    layers = fiona.listlayers(INPUT_GPKG)
    if len(layers) == 0:
        raise ValueError("No layers found in the GeoPackage.")
    layer_to_read = layers[0]
except Exception:
    layer_to_read = None  # geopandas will try default

gdf = gpd.read_file(INPUT_GPKG, layer=layer_to_read) if layer_to_read else gpd.read_file(INPUT_GPKG)

# Validate geometry as required (even though not used for tests)
gdf = gdf[gdf.geometry.notna()]
gdf = gdf[gdf.geometry.is_valid & ~gdf.geometry.is_empty].copy()

# ----------------------------
# Subset to hotspots vs non-hotspots
# Hotspot definition: GiP < 0.05 AND GiZ > 0
# ----------------------------
required_cols = ["GiP", "GiZ", "nearest_hw_dist_m", "hw_count_5km"]
missing = [c for c in required_cols if c not in gdf.columns]
if missing:
    raise KeyError(f"Missing required columns: {missing}. Available columns: {list(gdf.columns)}")

df = gdf[required_cols].copy()

# Ensure numeric and handle nulls
for c in required_cols:
    df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors="coerce")

df = df.dropna(subset=["GiP", "GiZ"]).copy()
df["is_hotspot"] = (df["GiP"] < 0.05) & (df["GiZ"] > 0)

# Prepare analysis subsets for each variable (drop nulls per variable)
def mannwhitney_summary(data: pd.DataFrame, value_col: str, group_col: str = "is_hotspot"):
    sub = data[[value_col, group_col]].dropna().copy()
    hotspot_vals = sub.loc[sub[group_col] == True, value_col].to_numpy()
    nonhot_vals = sub.loc[sub[group_col] == False, value_col].to_numpy()

    result = {
        "n_hotspot": int(hotspot_vals.size),
        "n_nonhotspot": int(nonhot_vals.size),
        "median_hotspot": float(np.nanmedian(hotspot_vals)) if hotspot_vals.size else None,
        "median_nonhotspot": float(np.nanmedian(nonhot_vals)) if nonhot_vals.size else None,
        "u_statistic": None,
        "p_value": None,
        "alternative": "two-sided",
    }

    if hotspot_vals.size == 0 or nonhot_vals.size == 0:
        return result

    # Mann–Whitney U test (two-sided)
    u = mannwhitneyu(hotspot_vals, nonhot_vals, alternative="two-sided", method="auto")
    result["u_statistic"] = float(u.statistic)
    result["p_value"] = float(u.pvalue)
    return result


results = {
    "hotspot_definition": {"GiP": "< 0.05", "GiZ": "> 0"},
    "overall_counts": {
        "n_total_valid_geom": int(len(gdf)),
        "n_total_with_GiP_GiZ": int(len(df)),
        "n_hotspot": int(df["is_hotspot"].sum()),
        "n_nonhotspot": int((~df["is_hotspot"]).sum()),
    },
    "tests": {
        "nearest_hw_dist_m": mannwhitney_summary(df, "nearest_hw_dist_m"),
        "hw_count_5km": mannwhitney_summary(df, "hw_count_5km"),
    },
}

# ----------------------------
# Save primary output JSON
# ----------------------------
with open(OUTPUT_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    json.dump(results, f, indent=2)

# ----------------------------
# Secondary output: comparative boxplots (optional manuscript artifact)
# ----------------------------
plot_df = df[["is_hotspot", "nearest_hw_dist_m", "hw_count_5km"]].copy()
plot_df["Group"] = np.where(plot_df["is_hotspot"], "Hotspot (GiP<0.05, GiZ>0)", "Other tracts")

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))

# nearest_hw_dist_m
tmp = plot_df[["Group", "nearest_hw_dist_m"]].dropna()
axes[0].boxplot(
    [tmp.loc[tmp["Group"].str.startswith("Hotspot"), "nearest_hw_dist_m"].to_numpy(),
     tmp.loc[~tmp["Group"].str.startswith("Hotspot"), "nearest_hw_dist_m"].to_numpy()],
    tick_labels=["Hotspot", "Other"],
    showfliers=False,
)
axes[0].set_title("Nearest HW distance (m)")
axes[0].set_ylabel("nearest_hw_dist_m")

# hw_count_5km
tmp = plot_df[["Group", "hw_count_5km"]].dropna()
axes[1].boxplot(
    [tmp.loc[tmp["Group"].str.startswith("Hotspot"), "hw_count_5km"].to_numpy(),
     tmp.loc[~tmp["Group"].str.startswith("Hotspot"), "hw_count_5km"].to_numpy()],
    tick_labels=["Hotspot", "Other"],
    showfliers=False,
)
axes[1].set_title("HW count within 5 km")
axes[1].set_ylabel("hw_count_5km")

plt.tight_layout()
fig_path = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "hotspot_vs_nonhotspot_proximity_boxplots.png")
plt.savefig(fig_path, dpi=200, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.close()

record_figure(
    output_dir=OUTPUT_DIR,
    file_path=fig_path,
    caption="Boxplots comparing nearest hardware distance and hardware counts within 5 km between significant asthma hotspots (Gi* p<0.05, z>0) and all other tracts.",
    role="promoted",
    objective_key="objective_1",
    step_index=1,
)

# Data Merging (Join, Merge, Spatial Join)

## Description

Combine two tables by a shared key (`pd.merge`) or by geometry (`gpd.sjoin`).
The correctness of every downstream step depends on the **match rate** — a
silent join that produces NaN for 50% of rows will poison later modeling.

## When to use

- Linking attribute data (CSV) to polygons (GeoPackage/SHP) on FIPS/GEOID.
- Combining variables from two CSV sources on a shared key.
- Enriching points/polygons via spatial containment.

## When NOT to use

- Reshape only (pivot/melt) — not a merge.
- Concatenating rows of identical schema — use `pd.concat`.

## Critical constraints

1. **Always cast join keys to string and `zfill` to a consistent width**
   before merging. Int-vs-string and missing-leading-zero mismatches silently
   drop every match.
   - State FIPS: `zfill(2)` (e.g. `"06"`).
   - County FIPS: `zfill(3)` (e.g. `"001"`).
   - County GEOID (state+county): `zfill(5)` (e.g. `"06001"`).
   - Tract GEOID: `zfill(11)`.
2. **Strip whitespace** on both keys: `.astype(str).str.strip()`.
3. **Replace Census sentinels with NaN** on any ACS/Decennial column:
   `df.replace(-666666666, np.nan, inplace=True)`. Values like `-999999999`,
   `-888888888`, `-666666666` are Census "suppressed / not available" codes
   — not real numbers.
4. **Audit and print match rate** after every merge. A match rate below 90%
   is a red flag — either keys are mis-formatted or the right-side dataset
   doesn't cover all left-side units (report both hypotheses).
5. **Left join keeps every left row** — NaN on the right side does NOT raise
   an error. You must inspect the output for NaN, not just row count.
6. **For spatial joins, both GeoDataFrames must be in the same projected
   CRS.** Reproject before `sjoin`.

## Paths

- `INPUT_PATH_LEFT`, `INPUT_PATH_RIGHT` — from the step's Input Node Paths.
- `OUTPUT_NODE_PATH` — merged primary output.
- `OUTPUT_DIR` — directory for join-audit log.

Never hardcode `"data.csv"`, `"dataset_a.csv"`, etc.

## Canonical example (CSV → GeoPackage left join on GEOID, with audit)

```python
import os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd

# ---- 1. Load ------------------------------------------------------------
left  = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH_LEFT)   # polygons (or a GeoDataFrame)
right = pd.read_csv(INPUT_PATH_RIGHT)    # attribute CSV

LEFT_KEY  = "<GEOID_COLUMN_ON_LEFT>"     # e.g. "GEOID"
RIGHT_KEY = "<GEOID_COLUMN_ON_RIGHT>"    # e.g. "GEOID"
KEY_WIDTH = 5                            # 2=state, 3=county, 5=county-GEOID, 11=tract

# ---- 2. Normalize join keys on BOTH sides -------------------------------
left[LEFT_KEY]   = left[LEFT_KEY].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(KEY_WIDTH)
right[RIGHT_KEY] = right[RIGHT_KEY].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(KEY_WIDTH)

# ---- 3. Clean Census sentinels on the right side ------------------------
for col in right.select_dtypes(include=["number", "object"]).columns:
    right[col] = pd.to_numeric(right[col], errors="ignore")
right = right.replace([-666666666, -888888888, -999999999], np.nan)

# ---- 4. Left join -------------------------------------------------------
merged = left.merge(right, left_on=LEFT_KEY, right_on=RIGHT_KEY, how="left")

# ---- 5. AUDIT the merge -------------------------------------------------
right_added = [c for c in right.columns if c != RIGHT_KEY and c in merged.columns]
audit_col   = right_added[0] if right_added else None

if audit_col is not None:
    matched      = int(merged[audit_col].notna().sum())
    total        = len(merged)
    match_rate   = matched / total if total else 0.0
    unmatched_n  = total - matched

    audit_lines = [
        f"[JOIN AUDIT] merged {total} left rows",
        f"  matched on '{audit_col}': {matched}/{total} ({match_rate:.1%})",
        f"  unmatched (NaN in right side): {unmatched_n}",
    ]
    if match_rate < 0.9:
        audit_lines.append(f"  WARNING: match rate below 90% — inspect keys:")
        audit_lines.append(f"    left  {LEFT_KEY} sample: {left[LEFT_KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
        audit_lines.append(f"    right {RIGHT_KEY} sample: {right[RIGHT_KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
        missing = merged.loc[merged[audit_col].isna(), LEFT_KEY].head(10).tolist()
        audit_lines.append(f"    example unmatched left keys: {missing}")
    audit = "\n".join(audit_lines)
    print(audit)
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    with open(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "join_audit.txt"), "w") as f:
        f.write(audit + "\n")

# ---- 6. Save primary output --------------------------------------------
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH), exist_ok=True)
if isinstance(merged, gpd.GeoDataFrame):
    merged.to_file(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH, driver="GPKG")
else:
    merged.to_csv(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH, index=False)
```

## Spatial join variant (points in polygons)

```python
import geopandas as gpd

points   = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH_LEFT)
polygons = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH_RIGHT)

# Reproject to same CRS (use polygons' CRS by convention)
if points.crs != polygons.crs:
    points = points.to_crs(polygons.crs)

joined = gpd.sjoin(points, polygons, how="left", predicate="within")

# Audit — how many points landed in a polygon?
unmatched = joined["index_right"].isna().sum()
print(f"[SJOIN AUDIT] {len(joined)-unmatched}/{len(joined)} points matched a polygon "
      f"({unmatched} outside all polygons)")
```

## Common pitfalls

| Pitfall | Fix |
|---|---|
| Int vs string FIPS silently produces 0 matches | Cast both sides to `str.strip().str.zfill(W)` with the right width. |
| Leading zeros stripped by `pd.read_csv` | Either `dtype={'GEOID': str}` at read time, or zfill after load. |
| ACS sentinel `-666666666` treated as a real value | `df.replace([-666666666, -888888888, -999999999], np.nan)`. |
| "58 records created" looks fine but half are NaN | Always print match rate, not just row count. |
| `sjoin` returns 0 rows | Reproject to a common CRS first; verify with `points.within(polygons.unary_union).sum()`. |
| `how="inner"` after a previously-failed join | Use `how="left"` for auditing; switch to inner only after you've verified the match rate. |
| Duplicated keys on the right side explode left row count | `right.drop_duplicates(subset=[RIGHT_KEY])` before merge if right is supposed to be unique. |
| Tract vs county GEOID width mismatch | Decide which level the join is at; enforce the matching width on both sides. |

]

if match_rate < 0.9:
    audit_lines.append("  WARNING: match rate below 90% — inspect GEOID formatting/coverage.")
    audit_lines.append(f"    left GEOID sample: {asthma_prox['GEOID'].head(5).tolist()}")
    audit_lines.append(f"    right GEOID sample: {acs['GEOID'].head(5).tolist()}")
    audit_lines.append(
        f"    example unmatched left GEOIDs: {merged.loc[merged[audit_col].isna(), 'GEOID'].head(10).tolist()}"
    )

audit_text = "\n".join(audit_lines)
with open(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "join_audit_asthma_proximity_acs.txt"), "w") as f:
    f.write(audit_text + "\n")

# ----------------------------
# Save output
# ----------------------------
merged.to_csv(OUTPUT_PATH, index=False)

# Data Merging (Join, Merge, Spatial Join)

## Description

Combine two tables by a shared key (`pd.merge`) or by geometry (`gpd.sjoin`).
The correctness of every downstream step depends on the **match rate** — a
silent join that produces NaN for 50% of rows will poison later modeling.

## When to use

- Linking attribute data (CSV) to polygons (GeoPackage/SHP) on FIPS/GEOID.
- Combining variables from two CSV sources on a shared key.
- Enriching points/polygons via spatial containment.

## When NOT to use

- Reshape only (pivot/melt) — not a merge.
- Concatenating rows of identical schema — use `pd.concat`.

## Critical constraints

1. **Always cast join keys to string and `zfill` to a consistent width**
   before merging. Int-vs-string and missing-leading-zero mismatches silently
   drop every match.
   - State FIPS: `zfill(2)` (e.g. `"06"`).
   - County FIPS: `zfill(3)` (e.g. `"001"`).
   - County GEOID (state+county): `zfill(5)` (e.g. `"06001"`).
   - Tract GEOID: `zfill(11)`.
2. **Strip whitespace** on both keys: `.astype(str).str.strip()`.
3. **Replace Census sentinels with NaN** on any ACS/Decennial column:
   `df.replace(-666666666, np.nan, inplace=True)`. Values like `-999999999`,
   `-888888888`, `-666666666` are Census "suppressed / not available" codes
   — not real numbers.
4. **Audit and print match rate** after every merge. A match rate below 90%
   is a red flag — either keys are mis-formatted or the right-side dataset
   doesn't cover all left-side units (report both hypotheses).
5. **Left join keeps every left row** — NaN on the right side does NOT raise
   an error. You must inspect the output for NaN, not just row count.
6. **For spatial joins, both GeoDataFrames must be in the same projected
   CRS.** Reproject before `sjoin`.

## Paths

- `INPUT_PATH_LEFT`, `INPUT_PATH_RIGHT` — from the step's Input Node Paths.
- `OUTPUT_NODE_PATH` — merged primary output.
- `OUTPUT_DIR` — directory for join-audit log.

Never hardcode `"data.csv"`, `"dataset_a.csv"`, etc.

## Canonical example (CSV → GeoPackage left join on GEOID, with audit)

```python
import os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd

# ---- 1. Load ------------------------------------------------------------
left  = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH_LEFT)   # polygons (or a GeoDataFrame)
right = pd.read_csv(INPUT_PATH_RIGHT)    # attribute CSV

LEFT_KEY  = "<GEOID_COLUMN_ON_LEFT>"     # e.g. "GEOID"
RIGHT_KEY = "<GEOID_COLUMN_ON_RIGHT>"    # e.g. "GEOID"
KEY_WIDTH = 5                            # 2=state, 3=county, 5=county-GEOID, 11=tract

# ---- 2. Normalize join keys on BOTH sides -------------------------------
left[LEFT_KEY]   = left[LEFT_KEY].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(KEY_WIDTH)
right[RIGHT_KEY] = right[RIGHT_KEY].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(KEY_WIDTH)

# ---- 3. Clean Census sentinels on the right side ------------------------
for col in right.select_dtypes(include=["number", "object"]).columns:
    right[col] = pd.to_numeric(right[col], errors="ignore")
right = right.replace([-666666666, -888888888, -999999999], np.nan)

# ---- 4. Left join -------------------------------------------------------
merged = left.merge(right, left_on=LEFT_KEY, right_on=RIGHT_KEY, how="left")

# ---- 5. AUDIT the merge -------------------------------------------------
right_added = [c for c in right.columns if c != RIGHT_KEY and c in merged.columns]
audit_col   = right_added[0] if right_added else None

if audit_col is not None:
    matched      = int(merged[audit_col].notna().sum())
    total        = len(merged)
    match_rate   = matched / total if total else 0.0
    unmatched_n  = total - matched

    audit_lines = [
        f"[JOIN AUDIT] merged {total} left rows",
        f"  matched on '{audit_col}': {matched}/{total} ({match_rate:.1%})",
        f"  unmatched (NaN in right side): {unmatched_n}",
    ]
    if match_rate < 0.9:
        audit_lines.append(f"  WARNING: match rate below 90% — inspect keys:")
        audit_lines.append(f"    left  {LEFT_KEY} sample: {left[LEFT_KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
        audit_lines.append(f"    right {RIGHT_KEY} sample: {right[RIGHT_KEY].head(5).tolist()}")
        missing = merged.loc[merged[audit_col].isna(), LEFT_KEY].head(10).tolist()
        audit_lines.append(f"    example unmatched left keys: {missing}")
    audit = "\n".join(audit_lines)
    print(audit)
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    with open(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "join_audit.txt"), "w") as f:
        f.write(audit + "\n")

# ---- 6. Save primary output --------------------------------------------
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH), exist_ok=True)
if isinstance(merged, gpd.GeoDataFrame):
    merged.to_file(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH, driver="GPKG")
else:
    merged.to_csv(OUTPUT_NODE_PATH, index=False)
```

## Spatial join variant (points in polygons)

```python
import geopandas as gpd

points   = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH_LEFT)
polygons = gpd.read_file(INPUT_PATH_RIGHT)

# Reproject to same CRS (use polygons' CRS by convention)
if points.crs != polygons.crs:
    points = points.to_crs(polygons.crs)

joined = gpd.sjoin(points, polygons, how="left", predicate="within")

# Audit — how many points landed in a polygon?
unmatched = joined["index_right"].isna().sum()
print(f"[SJOIN AUDIT] {len(joined)-unmatched}/{len(joined)} points matched a polygon "
      f"({unmatched} outside all polygons)")
```

## Common pitfalls

| Pitfall | Fix |
|---|---|
| Int vs string FIPS silently produces 0 matches | Cast both sides to `str.strip().str.zfill(W)` with the right width. |
| Leading zeros stripped by `pd.read_csv` | Either `dtype={'GEOID': str}` at read time, or zfill after load. |
| ACS sentinel `-666666666` treated as a real value | `df.replace([-666666666, -888888888, -999999999], np.nan)`. |
| "58 records created" looks fine but half are NaN | Always print match rate, not just row count. |
| `sjoin` returns 0 rows | Reproject to a common CRS first; verify with `points.within(polygons.unary_union).sum()`. |
| `how="inner"` after a previously-failed join | Use `how="left"` for auditing; switch to inner only after you've verified the match rate. |
| Duplicated keys on the right side explode left row count | `right.drop_duplicates(subset=[RIGHT_KEY])` before merge if right is supposed to be unique. |
| Tract vs county GEOID width mismatch | Decide which level the join is at; enforce the matching width on both sides. |

import os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

INPUT_PATH = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/tract_modeling_completecases.csv"
OUTPUT_PATH = "/bigdata/s0/tea5209/AGM/Outputs/c52bc37f-8e66-4df1-b20b-57c92bbd2311/tract_modeling_features.csv"
OUTPUT_DIR = os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_PATH)

# ----------------------------
# Load
# ----------------------------
df = pd.read_csv(INPUT_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"})

# Enforce expected columns (from get_columns)
required_cols = [
    "GEOID",
    "Asthma_prev",
    "nearest_hw_dist_m",
    "hw_count_5km",
    "ratio_poverty",
    "ratio_uninsured",
    "med_income",
    "ratio_black",
    "ratio_hispanic",
]
missing = [c for c in required_cols if c not in df.columns]
if missing:
    raise KeyError(f"Missing required columns: {missing}. Available columns: {df.columns.tolist()}")

# Normalize GEOID
df["GEOID"] = df["GEOID"].astype("string").str.strip()

# ----------------------------
# Numeric coercion
# (get_columns shows these are object; coerce to numeric)
# ----------------------------
num_cols = [
    "Asthma_prev",
    "nearest_hw_dist_m",
    "hw_count_5km",
    "ratio_poverty",
    "ratio_uninsured",
    "med_income",
    "ratio_black",
    "ratio_hispanic",
]
for c in num_cols:
    df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors="coerce")

# Replace common Census sentinels if present
df = df.replace([-666666666, -888888888, -999999999], np.nan)

# ----------------------------
# Feature engineering
# ----------------------------
# ln_nearest_hw_dist_m = ln(nearest_hw_dist_m + 1)
# Guard against negative distances (invalid); set to NaN so log isn't defined
df.loc[df["nearest_hw_dist_m"] < 0, "nearest_hw_dist_m"] = np.nan
df["ln_nearest_hw_dist_m"] = np.log(df["nearest_hw_dist_m"] + 1.0)

# Z-scores (population std, ddof=0); if std==0 or NaN, return NaN
def zscore(s: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    mu = s.mean(skipna=True)
    sigma = s.std(skipna=True, ddof=0)
    if pd.isna(sigma) or sigma == 0:
        return pd.Series(np.nan, index=s.index)
    return (s - mu) / sigma

df["z_ratio_poverty"] = zscore(df["ratio_poverty"])
df["z_ratio_uninsured"] = zscore(df["ratio_uninsured"])
df["z_med_income"] = zscore(df["med_income"])
df["z_ratio_black"] = zscore(df["ratio_black"])
df["z_ratio_hispanic"] = zscore(df["ratio_hispanic"])

# Interaction terms
df["ln_nearest_hw_dist_m_x_z_ratio_poverty"] = df["ln_nearest_hw_dist_m"] * df["z_ratio_poverty"]
df["ln_nearest_hw_dist_m_x_z_ratio_black"] = df["ln_nearest_hw_dist_m"] * df["z_ratio_black"]

# ----------------------------
# Save
# ----------------------------
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_PATH), exist_ok=True)
df.to_csv(OUTPUT_PATH, index=False)
